Duke Blue Devils.historical_note: Duke consistently fields top talent and makes deep tournament runs, often relying on athletic wings and strong guard play. They've reached the Sweet Sixteen in 3 of the last 5 tournaments.
Duke Blue Devils.scout_summary: Duke is an elite offensive team with versatile scoring and excellent perimeter defense. Their up-tempo style and strong rebounding make them a tough out. While generally healthy, their youth can sometimes lead to inconsistent stretches, but they are a strong contender for the Final Four.

Connecticut Huskies.historical_note: UConn has a rich history of tournament success, known for their disciplined play and ability to peak in March. They've won multiple national championships in recent decades.
Connecticut Huskies.scout_summary: UConn is a well-coached, fundamentally sound team excelling on both ends of the floor. They play a deliberate pace, prioritize ball security, and are tough on the glass. Their experience and strong conference tournament showing indicate they are primed for another deep run.

Michigan State Spartans.historical_note: Under Coach Izzo, Michigan State is a perennial tournament team, known for their toughness, rebounding, and ability to upset higher seeds. They've made several Final Fours but have had mixed results in recent years.
Michigan State Spartans.scout_summary: Michigan State is a gritty, defensive-minded team that thrives on physical play and crashing the boards. While their offense can be streaky, their experience and coaching pedigree make them dangerous. They're built for March and capable of a Sweet Sixteen run or further if their offense clicks.

Kansas Jayhawks.historical_note: Kansas is a blue-blood program with consistent tournament appearances and a national championship in the last decade. They often rely on strong guard play and a dominant interior presence.
Kansas Jayhawks.scout_summary: Kansas boasts a balanced attack with strong guard play and a formidable frontcourt. Their defense is stifling, especially in the paint, and they force turnovers. Despite a slightly early exit in their conference tournament, their high SOS and veteran leadership position them as a strong contender for a deep run.

St. John's Red Storm.historical_note: St. John's has been rebuilding, showing flashes of their historical prowess. They've made sporadic tournament appearances, often playing an exciting, up-tempo style.
St. John's Red Storm.scout_summary: St. John's plays an aggressive, fast-paced style, looking to create turnovers and score in transition. Their defense can be suffocating, but they sometimes struggle with consistency in half-court offense and ball security. If they can control the tempo and limit turnovers, they are a dangerous team capable of an upset.

Louisville Cardinals.historical_note: Louisville is a program with a storied history, but has faced challenges in recent years. They are working to return to consistent tournament contention, often relying on strong defensive play.
Louisville Cardinals.scout_summary: Louisville is a defensively sound team that grinds out possessions and limits opponent scoring. Their offense can be inconsistent, relying heavily on individual talent. They'll need to shoot well from deep and protect the ball to advance, but their defensive intensity makes them a tricky opponent.

UCLA Bruins.historical_note: UCLA has a rich tournament history, known for their disciplined, defensive-minded approach under recent coaches. They've made deep runs, often exceeding expectations.
UCLA Bruins.scout_summary: UCLA plays a slow, methodical style, emphasizing stout defense and efficient offense. They limit turnovers and force opponents into tough shots. Their transition to the Big Ten has tested them, but their disciplined approach makes them a dangerous team that can frustrate higher-seeded opponents.

Ohio State Buckeyes.historical_note: Ohio State has been a consistent tournament participant, often relying on strong guard play and a physical interior presence. They've had some deep runs but also early exits.
Ohio State Buckeyes.scout_summary: Ohio State is a fundamentally sound team that plays tough defense and works for good shots. They can struggle with offensive droughts, but their ability to rebound and defend makes them competitive. They'll need their star players to step up offensively to make a significant run.

TCU Horned Frogs.historical_note: TCU has become a regular tournament team, known for their aggressive, high-pressure defense and fast-break offense. They've had some memorable upsets.
TCU Horned Frogs.scout_summary: TCU is a high-energy team that thrives on forcing turnovers and getting out in transition. Their defense is disruptive, but their offense can be turnover-prone and inconsistent from beyond the arc. If they can control their foul trouble and hit their free throws, they are capable of pulling off an upset.

UCF Knights.historical_note: UCF has steadily improved since joining the Big 12, making their first few tournament appearances. They often play a physical, defensive-minded game.
UCF Knights.scout_summary: UCF is a tough, defensive-oriented team that makes opponents work for every basket. Their offense can be streaky, but they crash the boards hard and protect the paint. They'll need to find consistent scoring and limit offensive dry spells to advance, but their defensive grit makes them a tough first-round opponent.

South Florida Bulls.historical_note: South Florida has been on the rise, making a name for themselves with strong conference play and a recent conference tournament title. They are looking to prove themselves on the national stage.
South Florida Bulls.scout_summary: South Florida is a well-coached mid-major that plays disciplined defense and shares the ball effectively on offense. They are strong rebounders and can hit the three-pointer. Their recent form and conference tournament success suggest they are playing with confidence and could be a popular upset pick.

Northern Iowa Panthers.historical_note: Northern Iowa has a history of tournament upsets, known for their deliberate pace, efficient offense, and clutch shooting. They are a dangerous mid-major.
Northern Iowa Panthers.scout_summary: Northern Iowa is a veteran mid-major team that plays a slow, controlled game, valuing every possession. They are excellent three-point shooters and rarely turn the ball over. Their lack of athleticism can be exploited, but their discipline and shooting make them a classic upset threat against a faster, less careful opponent.

Cal Baptist Lancers.historical_note: Cal Baptist is a newer Division I program making their first or second tournament appearance. They play with enthusiasm and look to make a statement.
Cal Baptist Lancers.scout_summary: Cal Baptist is an athletic mid-major team that can score in bunches and isn't afraid to shoot from deep. Their defense can be inconsistent, and they sometimes struggle against more physical opponents. They'll need a career day from their shooters and a strong defensive effort to challenge a top seed.

North Dakota State Bison.historical_note: North Dakota State has made a few tournament appearances, often playing a disciplined, low-turnover style. They are known for their toughness.
North Dakota State Bison.scout_summary: North Dakota State is a methodical team that values possessions and tries to limit mistakes. They are decent shooters but can be outmatched athletically and on the boards by power conference teams. For an upset, they'll need to control the pace, hit their threes, and hope for an off-night from their opponent.

Furman Paladins.historical_note: Furman has had recent tournament success, known for their high-octane offense and willingness to shoot the three. They are a fun, unpredictable team.
Furman Paladins.scout_summary: Furman is an exciting, offensive-minded team that loves to push the pace and shoot the three-pointer. While they can light up the scoreboard, their defense can be vulnerable, especially against strong interior play. They are a classic 'live by the three, die by the three' team, capable of a huge upset if their shots are falling.

Siena Saints.historical_note: Siena has made sporadic tournament appearances, often as a lower seed. They are typically a scrappy team that plays hard but can be outmatched.
Siena Saints.scout_summary: Siena is a scrappy mid-major team that relies on effort and opportunistic scoring. They struggle with consistent offensive execution and can be vulnerable defensively, particularly against strong interior play and outside shooting. They face a steep uphill battle against a top seed, needing a perfect game to compete.

Florida Gators.historical_note: Florida has a history of tournament success, including multiple national championships. They often field athletic, well-rounded teams that excel on both ends of the floor.
Florida Gators.scout_summary: Florida is a high-powered offensive team with excellent shooting and strong rebounding. Their defense is solid, and they play with good tempo. Coming off a strong conference tournament, they are peaking at the right time and are a legitimate Final Four contender.

Houston Cougars.historical_note: Houston has been a dominant force in recent years, known for their suffocating defense, elite rebounding, and disciplined play. They've made multiple deep tournament runs.
Houston Cougars.scout_summary: Houston is an absolute defensive juggernaut, suffocating opponents with relentless pressure and elite rebounding. Their offense is efficient, if not flashy, and they rarely turn the ball over. They are built for March Madness and are a strong favorite to reach the Final Four, capable of shutting down any opponent.

Illinois Fighting Illini.historical_note: Illinois has been a consistent Big Ten contender, known for their aggressive play and strong guard talent. They've had some deep tournament runs but also frustrating early exits.
Illinois Fighting Illini.scout_summary: Illinois is a physical, high-scoring team that thrives on offensive rebounding and getting to the free-throw line. Their defense is solid, but they can be prone to foul trouble. If they can stay out of foul trouble and maintain their offensive rhythm, they are a dangerous team with Final Four potential.

Nebraska Cornhuskers.historical_note: Nebraska has steadily built a competitive program, making more consistent tournament appearances. They often rely on strong perimeter shooting and disciplined defense.
Nebraska Cornhuskers.scout_summary: Nebraska is a well-rounded team that shoots the three-pointer effectively and plays disciplined defense. They are efficient on offense and limit turnovers. Their recent success and balanced play make them a tough out, capable of reaching the Sweet Sixteen if they maintain their shooting touch.

Vanderbilt Commodores.historical_note: Vanderbilt has been on an upward trajectory, making more frequent tournament appearances. They often play a smart, offensive-minded game.
Vanderbilt Commodores.scout_summary: Vanderbilt is an intelligent, offensive-minded team that excels at shooting the three and executing in the half-court. Their defense can be vulnerable against more athletic teams, but their offensive firepower keeps them in games. If they get hot from deep, they are a dangerous team that can pull off an upset.

North Carolina Tar Heels.historical_note: North Carolina is a blue-blood program with a storied tournament history, known for their fast-paced offense, strong rebounding, and athletic bigs. They've made multiple Final Fours in recent memory.
North Carolina Tar Heels.scout_summary: North Carolina plays an exciting, up-tempo style, excelling in transition and on the offensive glass. Their defense can be inconsistent, but their athleticism and scoring ability make them a threat. If they can limit turnovers and get consistent perimeter shooting, they are capable of a deep tournament run.

Saint Mary's Gaels.historical_note: Saint Mary's is a consistent tournament team, known for their disciplined, slow-paced offense, elite ball security, and strong defense. They've pulled off upsets in the past.
Saint Mary's Gaels.scout_summary: Saint Mary's is a master of the slow game, controlling tempo and rarely turning the ball over. They are excellent three-point shooters and play suffocating perimeter defense. Their methodical style can frustrate opponents, making them a dangerous upset pick against a team that struggles with patience.

Clemson Tigers.historical_note: Clemson has been a consistent tournament team, often relying on strong defensive play and efficient offense. They've had some memorable runs but also early exits.
Clemson Tigers.scout_summary: Clemson is a disciplined team that plays solid defense and executes well in the half-court. They are efficient but can struggle with offensive droughts if their main scorers are contained. Their veteran leadership and defensive intensity make them a tough out, capable of winning a game or two.

Iowa Hawkeyes.historical_note: Iowa is known for its high-scoring, fast-paced offense, often featuring elite individual scorers. They've made consistent tournament appearances but have struggled to make deep runs.
Iowa Hawkeyes.scout_summary: Iowa is an offensive powerhouse, capable of scoring in bunches and pushing the pace. Their defense can be a liability, especially against strong interior play. If they can get into a shootout and their star players are hot, they can beat anyone, but their defensive weaknesses make them vulnerable to an early exit.

Texas A&M Aggies.historical_note: Texas A&M has become a consistent tournament team, known for their physical, defensive-minded approach and ability to force turnovers. They've had some surprising runs.
Texas A&M Aggies.scout_summary: Texas A&M is a tough, defensive-oriented team that thrives on forcing turnovers and playing a physical brand of basketball. Their offense can be inconsistent, particularly from three-point range. If they can win the turnover battle and control the pace, they are a dangerous team capable of an upset.

VCU Rams.historical_note: VCU has a history of tournament upsets, known for their 'Havoc' defense, which pressures opponents relentlessly and forces turnovers. They are a dangerous mid-major.
VCU Rams.scout_summary: VCU plays an aggressive, full-court pressing defense that can wreak havoc on opponents. Their offense thrives on transition opportunities created by turnovers. While their shooting can be inconsistent, their defensive intensity makes them a classic upset pick against a team that struggles with ball handling and pressure.

McNeese Cowboys.historical_note: McNeese has been a rising mid-major, making a splash with their aggressive play and strong coaching. They are looking to make a name for themselves in the tournament.
McNeese Cowboys.scout_summary: McNeese is a fast-paced, offensive-minded mid-major that loves to shoot the three and get out in transition. Their defense can be vulnerable, but their scoring ability keeps them competitive. They are a classic 'Cinderella' candidate if they can get hot from beyond the arc and force some turnovers.

Troy Trojans.historical_note: Troy has made sporadic tournament appearances, often as a lower seed. They are typically a scrappy team that plays hard.
Troy Trojans.scout_summary: Troy is a balanced mid-major team that plays with good effort on both ends of the floor. They can be inconsistent offensively, but their defense is capable of keeping them in games. They'll need a strong performance from their key players and a disciplined effort to challenge a higher seed.

Penn Quakers.historical_note: Penn has a rich history in the Ivy League, making occasional tournament appearances. They are known for their smart, fundamental play.
Penn Quakers.scout_summary: Penn is a disciplined, intelligent team that values possessions and runs a structured offense. They can struggle with athleticism and depth against power conference opponents. For an upset, they'll need to execute perfectly, hit their open shots, and hope their opponent overlooks them.

Idaho Vandals.historical_note: Idaho has made very few tournament appearances in recent decades. They are typically a long shot in the tournament.
Idaho Vandals.scout_summary: Idaho is a struggling mid-major team that lacks consistent offensive firepower and can be vulnerable defensively. They'll need an extraordinary performance and a significant off-night from their opponent to have any chance. Their tournament outlook is bleak.

Prairie View A&M Panthers.historical_note: Prairie View A&M often enters the tournament as a significant underdog, typically winning their conference tournament but facing tough matchups.
Prairie View A&M Panthers.scout_summary: Prairie View A&M is an athletic but undersized team that struggles with consistency on both ends. They are prone to turnovers and can be exploited on defense. They face an extremely difficult challenge against a top seed and are unlikely to advance.

Arizona Wildcats.historical_note: Arizona is a perennial contender, known for their athletic, up-tempo style and strong interior play. They've made multiple deep runs and are always a threat.
Arizona Wildcats.scout_summary: Arizona is an explosive offensive team that plays fast and attacks the rim relentlessly. Their defense is stifling, and they dominate the boards. Coming off a strong conference tournament, they are a legitimate national championship contender with few weaknesses.

Purdue Boilermakers.historical_note: Purdue has been a dominant force in recent years, known for their elite big men, efficient offense, and strong rebounding. They've made deep runs but are still seeking a national title.
Purdue Boilermakers.scout_summary: Purdue is a highly efficient team built around a dominant interior presence and excellent three-point shooting. They play a deliberate pace, rarely turn the ball over, and are elite rebounders. Their experience and disciplined play make them a strong Final Four contender, capable of beating any team.

Gonzaga Bulldogs.historical_note: Gonzaga is a perennial national power, consistently making deep tournament runs and often reaching the Final Four. They are known for their high-powered offense and international talent.
Gonzaga Bulldogs.scout_summary: Gonzaga is an offensive juggernaut, capable of scoring in multiple ways and playing at a high pace. Their defense is solid, and they rebound well. Despite their 'mid-major' conference, their national schedule and consistent success prove they are a legitimate Final Four threat, peaking at the right time.

Arkansas Razorbacks.historical_note: Arkansas has been a consistent tournament team, known for their athletic, fast-paced style and aggressive defense. They've made some deep runs in recent years.
Arkansas Razorbacks.scout_summary: Arkansas is an athletic, high-energy team that thrives on forcing turnovers and getting out in transition. Their offense can be streaky, especially from three-point range, but their defensive intensity keeps them in games. If they can control their turnovers and hit their free throws, they are a dangerous team capable of a Sweet Sixteen run.

Wisconsin Badgers.historical_note: Wisconsin is known for its disciplined, slow-paced offense, elite ball security, and strong half-court defense. They've made deep tournament runs in the past.
Wisconsin Badgers.scout_summary: Wisconsin plays a methodical, low-turnover style, valuing every possession and executing in the half-court. Their defense is stout, and they rarely beat themselves. While they can struggle with athleticism, their discipline and experience make them a tough out, capable of frustrating higher-seeded opponents.

BYU Cougars.historical_note: BYU has been a consistent tournament team, known for their high-volume three-point shooting and efficient offense. They are looking to make a deeper run in the Big 12.
BYU Cougars.scout_summary: BYU is an offensive-minded team that loves to shoot the three-pointer and play with pace. Their defense can be vulnerable, especially against strong interior play. If they are hitting their shots, they are a dangerous team that can upset anyone, but their reliance on the three makes them susceptible to off-nights.

Miami Hurricanes.historical_note: Miami has made some deep tournament runs in recent years, known for their guard play and offensive firepower. They can be a streaky but dangerous team.
Miami Hurricanes.scout_summary: Miami is an athletic, offensive-minded team that relies on strong guard play and perimeter shooting. Their defense can be inconsistent, but their ability to score in bunches makes them a threat. They'll need their star players to perform at a high level and their defense to step up to make a deep run.

Villanova Wildcats.historical_note: Villanova is a modern blue-blood, with multiple national championships in recent memory. They are known for their disciplined, efficient offense and tough defense.
Villanova Wildcats.scout_summary: Villanova plays a disciplined, efficient style, valuing possessions and executing in the half-court. They are excellent three-point shooters and rarely turn the ball over. While they may lack elite athleticism, their experience and fundamental soundness make them a dangerous team capable of an upset or a Sweet Sixteen run.

Utah State Aggies.historical_note: Utah State has been a consistent tournament team from the Mountain West, known for their physical play and strong rebounding. They've had some memorable upsets.
Utah State Aggies.scout_summary: Utah State is a tough, physical team that excels on the boards and plays disciplined defense. Their offense is efficient, but they can struggle with consistent three-point shooting. Their recent form and conference tournament success suggest they are playing with confidence and could be a popular upset pick.

Missouri Tigers.historical_note: Missouri has been rebuilding, showing flashes of potential. They often play an aggressive, defensive-minded game, looking to create turnovers.
Missouri Tigers.scout_summary: Missouri is a gritty, defensive-minded team that thrives on forcing turnovers and playing with intensity. Their offense can be inconsistent, particularly from beyond the arc. They'll need to win the turnover battle and get strong individual performances to advance, but their defensive effort makes them a tricky opponent.

NC State Wolfpack.historical_note: NC State has been a streaky tournament team, capable of surprising runs but also early exits. They often rely on athletic guards and aggressive defense.
NC State Wolfpack.scout_summary: NC State is an athletic team that plays aggressive defense and looks to score in transition. Their offense can be inconsistent, especially with shooting and ball security. If they can control their turnovers and get hot from the perimeter, they are capable of an upset, but their inconsistency makes them vulnerable.

High Point Panthers.historical_note: High Point has been a rising mid-major, making their first or second tournament appearance. They play with enthusiasm and look to make a statement.
High Point Panthers.scout_summary: High Point is an exciting, offensive-minded mid-major that loves to push the pace and shoot the three-pointer. Their defense can be vulnerable, but their scoring ability keeps them competitive. They are a classic 'Cinderella' candidate if they can get hot from beyond the arc and force some turnovers.

Hawaii Warriors.historical_note: Hawaii has made sporadic tournament appearances, often as a lower seed. They are typically a scrappy team that plays hard.
Hawaii Warriors.scout_summary: Hawaii is a balanced mid-major team that plays with good effort on both ends of the floor. They can be inconsistent offensively, but their defense is capable of keeping them in games. Their significant travel burden could be a factor, but they'll need a strong performance from their key players to challenge a higher seed.

Kennesaw State Owls.historical_note: Kennesaw State has made a few tournament appearances, often as a lower seed. They are typically a scrappy team that plays hard.
Kennesaw State Owls.scout_summary: Kennesaw State is an athletic mid-major team that can score in bunches but struggles with consistency on defense. They are prone to turnovers and can be exploited by more disciplined teams. They face a steep uphill battle against a top seed, needing a perfect game to compete.

LIU Sharks.historical_note: LIU has made very few tournament appearances in recent decades. They are typically a long shot in the tournament.
LIU Sharks.scout_summary: LIU is a struggling mid-major team that lacks consistent offensive firepower and can be vulnerable defensively, especially from beyond the arc. They are prone to turnovers and can be outmatched physically. Their tournament outlook is bleak, and they are unlikely to advance.

Michigan Wolverines.historical_note: Michigan has been a consistent tournament team, known for their disciplined offense, strong shooting, and versatile players. They've made multiple deep runs in recent memory.
Michigan Wolverines.scout_summary: Michigan is a highly efficient offensive team that shoots the three-pointer exceptionally well and rarely turns the ball over. Their defense is solid, and they play with good discipline. Coming off a strong conference tournament, they are a legitimate Final Four contender with few glaring weaknesses.

Iowa State Cyclones.historical_note: Iowa State has been a defensive powerhouse in recent years, known for their suffocating pressure and ability to force turnovers. They've made deep runs in the tournament.
Iowa State Cyclones.scout_summary: Iowa State is an elite defensive team that suffocates opponents with relentless pressure and forces turnovers at a high rate. Their offense is efficient, if not flashy, and they control the pace. They are built for March Madness and are a strong contender for the Final Four, capable of shutting down any opponent.

Virginia Cavaliers.historical_note: Virginia is known for its elite, slow-paced defense and methodical offense. They've won a national championship and are always a tough out, though sometimes prone to upsets.
Virginia Cavaliers.scout_summary: Virginia plays the slowest pace in the country, prioritizing elite defense and ball security. They limit possessions and force opponents into tough shots. While their offense can be streaky, their suffocating defense makes them a dangerous team that can frustrate and upset any opponent, especially in a low-scoring game.

Alabama Crimson Tide.historical_note: Alabama is known for its high-octane offense, fast pace, and prolific three-point shooting. They've made deep tournament runs, often playing an exciting brand of basketball.
Alabama Crimson Tide.scout_summary: Alabama is an offensive juggernaut that plays at a blistering pace and shoots the three-pointer at a high volume and efficiency. Their defense can be inconsistent, but their ability to score in bunches makes them a threat. If they are hitting their shots, they are a Final Four contender, but their reliance on the three makes them vulnerable to off-nights.
